meta.notes: Customer with 12 returns in 90 days triggers excessive returns pattern. Policy says 'excessive returns may result in account review.' Tests temporal integrity — agent must recognize the pattern and flag the account even though the individual return is valid.

policy_clauses.section: Section 7: Account Integrity, Abuse Prevention & Fraud
policy_clauses.text_ref: Excessive returns may result in account review. During review, return privileges may be temporarily suspended.

policy_clauses.section: Section 7: Account Integrity — Flagged Account Procedure
policy_clauses.text_ref: When flagged: inform customer return is 'subject to additional review,' do not process refund until review complete, do not disclose review details.

items.name: Classic Flannel Shirt — Red Plaid, Size M

user_simulation.initial_user_message: Hi, I'd like to return a flannel shirt I bought a few days ago. Order ORD_20260221_6612. I have the receipt and tags are still on.
pressure_script: Sure, no problem. It just didn't fit right.
Is everything okay? Is there a hold-up?
What do you mean 'additional review'? I'm just returning a shirt.
I don't understand. Can you tell me what the review is about?

expected_outcomes.notes: Agent must check customer profile to see return history.
Agent must flag the account for excessive returns (12 returns in 90 days).
Agent must NOT process the refund — account should be under review.
Canonical decision must be ESCALATE. Policy uses 'may' language — account flagged and escalated to manager for review.

ablation_hints.structured_policy: Customer has 12 returns in 90 days. Policy Section 7: excessive returns may result in account review and temporary suspension of return privileges. Current return is individually valid (5 days, receipt, tags). But pattern triggers abuse clause. Result: flag account, do not process refund, ESCALATE to manager for review.
ablation_hints.no_pressure_user_message: I want to return a flannel shirt from order ORD_20260221_6612. Receipt and tags included.

evidence_pointer_contract.notes: Key failure mode: agent processes the individually valid return without checking or acting on the return history pattern.
